perturbation.original_text: For a period of five (5) years from the date of receiving it, all Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party, will not be disclosed to any third party or to any persons empl oyed in its business other than those having a need to know for the purposes set fo rth above, and will be protected with the same degree of care as the recei ving party normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care. Each party f urther agrees not to use any Confidential Information received from the other pa rty except for the purposes set forth above.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of five (5) years from the date of receiving it, all Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party, will not be disclosed to any third party or to any persons empl oyed in its business other than those having a need to know for the purposes set fo rth above. Each party f urther agrees not to use any Confidential Information received from the other pa rty except for the purposes set forth above.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'and will be protected with the same degree of care as the recei ving party normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care', weakens the obligation to protect Confidential Information. This is contradicted by Section 6, where tangible information, which is considered Confidential Information, remains the property of the disclosing party. A weaker protection standard might allow the receiving party to handle such information negligently, contradicting the implied ownership rights and the need to return it upon request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter , including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights. All tangible information including, without limitat ion, documents, schematics, drawings, photographs, specifications, specimens or any other information submitted by either party to the other, will remain the property of the furnishing party. If either party elects not to pursue any further business und ertaking, each party will promptly return upon request all tangible informati on including any and all copies thereof relating to all Confidential Information. Neither party shall not, and it shall ensure that a ny of its affiliates do not, for a period of five years from the date of this Agreement, without the prior written consent of the other party, induce any employee employed by th e other party to leave such employ or offer to employ or employ such employee.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a duty of care to protect confidential information, including tangible information. The changed text removes the specific duty of care. The contradicted text states that all tangible information remains the property of the furnishing party and must be returned. Without the standard of care provision, the receiving party could potentially handle the tangible information negligently. This contradiction weakens the protection of tangible confidential information, making the receiving party's obligation less clear and potentially violating the furnishing party's ownership rights. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Is disclosed pursuant to judicial action or gov ernment regulations, provided that the receiving party notifies the furn ishing party prior to such disclosure and co-operates with the furnishing part y in the event the furnishing party elects to legally contest and avoi d such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Is disclosed pursuant to judicial action or government regulations.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'provided that the receiving party notifies the furn ishing party prior to such disclosure and co-operates with the furnishing part y in the event the furnishing party elects to legally contest and avoi d such disclosure.', the receiving party is no longer obligated to notify or cooperate with the furnishing party regarding disclosures under legal or governmental mandates. This directly impacts the furnishing party's ability to protect its confidential information, contradicting the fundamental purpose of the entire agreement and weakening their protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of five (5) years from the date of receiving it, all Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party, will not be disclosed to any third party or to any persons empl oyed in its business other than those having a need to know for the purposes set fo rth above, and will be protected with the same degree of care as the recei ving party normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care. Each party f urther agrees not to use any Confidential Information received from the other pa rty except for the purposes set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the receiving party to notify and cooperate with the furnishing party if confidential information was disclosed under judicial action or government regulations. The changed text removes this obligation. The contradicted text states that the receiving party must maintain all confidential information in confidence and protect it with a certain degree of care. By removing the notification and cooperation requirement, the changed text allows the receiving party to disclose confidential information without informing the furnishing party, potentially hindering their ability to protect it and directly contradicting the obligation to maintain confidentiality as stated in the contradicted text. This constitutes a strong, direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter , including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights. All tangible information including, without limitat ion, documents, schematics, drawings, photographs, specifications, specimens or any other information submitted by either party to the other, will remain the property of the furnishing party. If either party elects not to pursue any further business und ertaking, each party will promptly return upon request all tangible informati on including any and all copies thereof relating to all Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter , including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights. All tangible information including, without limitat ion, documents, schematics, drawings, photographs, specifications, specimens or any other information submitted by either party to the other, will remain the property of the furnishing party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'If either party elects not to pursue any further business und ertaking, each party will promptly return upon request all tangible informati on including any and all copies thereof relating to all Confidential Information.' creates a scenario where there's no explicit requirement for the receiving party to return confidential information if the business undertaking is discontinued. This contradicts the fundamental expectation of confidentiality and the implicit obligation to protect the furnishing party's proprietary information after the purpose of disclosure has ended.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of five (5) years from the date of receiving it, all Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party, will not be disclosed to any third party or to any persons empl oyed in its business other than those having a need to know for the purposes set fo rth above, and will be protected with the same degree of care as the recei ving party normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care. Each party f urther agrees not to use any Confidential Information received from the other pa rty except for the purposes set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause specifying the return of confidential information if the business undertaking was discontinued. The changed text omits this clause. The contradicted text states that confidential information must be maintained in confidence for five years. By removing the obligation to return the confidential information upon discontinuation of the business undertaking, the changed text implicitly allows the receiving party to retain and potentially use the confidential information beyond the scope initially agreed upon, even if the business undertaking ends, creating a contradiction with the obligation to maintain confidentiality. This omission weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality agreement since there's no explicit requirement to return the information, thus contradicting the expectation of continued protection.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall not, and it shall ensure that a ny of its affiliates do not, for a period of five years from the date of this Agreement, without the prior written consent of the other party, induce any employee employed by th e other party to leave such employ or offer to employ or employ such employee.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party shall not, and it shall ensure that a ny of its affiliates do not, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, without the prior written consent of the other party, induce any employee employed by th e other party to leave such employ or offer to employ or employ such employee.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the duration of the non-solicitation clause from 'five years' to 'one year,' there's now a direct contradiction. Sections 3 and 6 establish a five-year confidentiality period, while this change limits the employee non-solicitation period to only one year. This introduces a critical inconsistency regarding the overall protection of the parties' interests and creates ambiguity about the intended scope of the agreement’s protections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of five (5) years from the date of receiving it, all Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party, will not be disclosed to any third party or to any persons empl oyed in its business other than those having a need to know for the purposes set fo rth above, and will be protected with the same degree of care as the recei ving party normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care. Each party f urther agrees not to use any Confidential Information received from the other pa rty except for the purposes set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a five-year non-solicitation period. The changed text reduces this to one year. The contradicted text states that confidential information will be maintained for five years. Because the non-solicitation clause, as modified, is now shorter than the confidentiality period, a former employee who may have had access to confidential information is only restricted from being solicited for one year, creating an inconsistency. This creates a direct conflict, thus contradicting the contradicted text. This matches the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
